titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L253
GA L253
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was initially made by the Byzantine Text of John Project in 2004-7, led by Roderic L. Mullen, and later adopted for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John.

The IGNTP transcription file was duplicated for the standalone version of the Byzantine Text of John on 9.7.2014. This means that there may be slight discrepancies between the 2007 file used to prepared the Byzantine apparatus and the 2014 transcription in the present file.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Word break and corrector tags at 18.39 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 20.23* corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 15.10 (in C*) corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks at 16.24 and 16.25 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Num tag changed to Nom Sac tag at 18.14 by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 253 (St. Ρetersburg, Russian Νat. Lib., Gr. 71)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΤype Lsk (Saturday-Sunday rdgs only)Greek hand seems very unsure at times, (early) Latin hand at top of pagesF 168r -- later note-- ex museo Ρetri Dubrowsky Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 01.07.02Τranscription finished: 05.11.02Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 01.07.02Τransference to Collate finished: 05.11.02File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 05.11.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 15.11.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 23.10.13 End date: 27.10.13 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes. Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No. 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks: Unidentified reading 1 is on F162v: John 10,11-16.

div.note: Our lectionary index has Luke 24,12-35 on this day.
Our lectionary index has John 3,1-15 on this day.
Our lectionary index has John 2,12-22 on this day.

ab.note: The S1 W2 D1 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the R9 (εωθινον θ) reading.

div.note: John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 11,27 is added to the end of the lection in this manuscript.
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)

ab.note: The S1 W7 D7 reading (John 21,14-25) is cross-referenced to the R11 αναστασιμον [num]ια[\num] reading.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
One of a number of Pericopes for Various Occasions not mentioned in the Menologion.
It is rare for a Synaxarion pericope to appear at the end of the lectionary and so it is uncertain whether it has been tagged correctly.
It is rare for a Synaxarion pericope to appear at the end of the lectionary and so it is uncertain whether it has been tagged correctly.
It is rare for a Synaxarion pericope to appear at the end of the lectionary and so it is uncertain whether it has been tagged correctly.
It is rare for a Synaxarion pericope to appear at the end of the lectionary and so it is uncertain whether it has been tagged correctly.
